Event: Develops cocaine addiction during VP arc
Explanation: Addiction harms his judgment and performance with no benefit shown.

Event: Implements impractical, flashy website features as VP
Explanation: His obsession with “cool” features undermines a needed upgrade and frustrates others.

Event: Creates WUPHF as a social app instead of emergency-alert service
Explanation: He wastes a potentially valuable technology on a trivial, unviable use-case.

Event: Returns to Scranton for Dwight and Angela’s wedding
Explanation: His attendance has no described negative consequences and mildly fulfills social expectations.

Event: Acts as a bad boss: arrogant bullying and unethical behavior as VP
Explanation: He enjoys power and status short term while harming subordinates.

Event: Continues provoking Jim while slacking and playing Tetris
Explanation: His defiance gains him nothing and leads directly to public humiliation and demotion to the closet office.

Event: Humiliates Ryan by moving him to a closet office
Explanation: Jim restores control and gets Ryan to work but does so via public humiliation.

Event: Repeatedly dumps Kelly when he has better options
Explanation: He preserves his freedom at the cost of Kelly’s feelings and stability.

Event: Pursues Kelly again whenever she moves on
Explanation: He regains ego gratification while undermining her healthier relationships.

Event: Loudly proclaims “How’s my favorite branch doing?” insincerely
Explanation: He burnishes his image with shallow charm that annoys others slightly.

Event: Tells everyone to double-count their sales as website sales
Explanation: Short-term cosmetic gain leads directly to fraud exposure and career ruin.

Event: Throws his weight around with former coworkers after promotion
Explanation: He indulges his ego and authority while worsening others’ work environment.

Event: Helps Creed by giving him a fake “blog” as a Word doc
Explanation: He avoids trouble and arguably protects others from Creed’s disturbing posts.

Event: Feigns reform at Scranton while maintaining revenge list
Explanation: He regains a job but wastes energy plotting harm to people who helped rehire him.

Event: Feeds a strawberry to an allergic baby
Explanation: He deliberately endangers the child’s health for no gain beyond spiteful distraction.

Event: Abandons the allergic baby to run off with Kelly
Explanation: He jeopardizes the child and Kelly’s stability while gaining only a fragile, toxic reunion.

Event: Confirms Angela’s boyfriend is gay from late-night likes
Explanation: He accurately interprets behavior, mildly helping clarify a social truth with no shown harm.

Event: Sets up a fraudulent corporate website launch with flashy livestream
Explanation: The showy launch serves his image briefly but accelerates scrutiny of a bad product.

Event: Gets Kelly to dump Darryl and give him money to travel
Explanation: He gains freedom and cash while leaving Kelly worse off and hurting Darryl.

Event: Endangers office jobs with restructuring plan favoring website
Explanation: He advances his tech-bro vision and personal stature while risking coworkers’ livelihoods.

Event: Forces staff to work overtime crediting their sales to the site
Explanation: He pads his metrics and keeps his project afloat while overworking and deceiving employees.

Event: Goes to parties and takes drugs while staff stay late
Explanation: He damages his own health and credibility while demoralizing those stuck doing his work.

Event: Gleefully ends relationship with Kelly when accepting corporate job
Explanation: He escapes a relationship he doesn’t value, leaving Kelly emotionally hurt.

Event: Gives Jim a verbal warning partly out of spite
Explanation: He asserts authority and vents insecurity while unfairly targeting a competent coworker.

Event: Fails to prepare sound expense projections for Michael’s startup
Explanation: His flawed projections risk the business and display his incompetence, undercutting his own status.

Event: Misleads Dunder Mifflin shareholders with falsified results
Explanation: The fraud temporarily masks failure but leads to jail and career destruction.

Event: Accepts obviously unsuitable VP promotion and overreaches
Explanation: He gains short-term prestige at the cost of legal trouble and company instability.

Event: Hooks up with Kelly in the finale by sabotaging her stable life with Ravi
Explanation: He regains Kelly while wrecking her better relationship and humiliating Ravi.

Event: Tells Kelly he has no long-term plans and doesn’t regret hurting her
Explanation: He preserves his independence while deeply upsetting her.
